disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A firm, hyperkeratotic (hard, rough) raised lump on the plantar surface that has slowly enlarged over time
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the lump on your foot?
How long has it been there and has it changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a hard, rough little lump on the bottom of my foot. It's been there a while and it's slowly got bigger and rougher.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is elicited more by lateral compression (pinching the sides of the lesion) than by direct downward pressure on it
trigger_question_examples: Does it hurt more when you press straight down on it, or when you squeeze it from the sides?
What exactly brings the sharp pain on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you say it — it's worse when I pinch or squeeze the sides of it than when I press straight down on the top.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Regular use of communal showers at a public pool
trigger_question_examples: Do you walk barefoot in any shared or public places, like a gym or pool?
Tell me a bit about your day-to-day — any swimming or shared showers?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I swim a lot and I always use the communal showers at the public pool, usually barefoot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tried an over-the-counter corn plaster with minimal relief
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything for it yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I stuck one of those corn plasters on it from the chemist but it barely did anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no redness spreading up the foot, and no diabetes
trigger_question_examples: Any fever, or redness spreading up your foot or leg?
Do you have diabetes or any problems with circulation or numbness in your feet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, and nothing spreading up my foot. I don't have diabetes and my feet are normally fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone and is at risk of falling because of an antalgic (pain-avoiding) gait
trigger_question_examples: How are you managing at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm on my own and I'm hopping everywhere holding the furniture — I'm a bit scared I'll take a tumble.
